openmeter/progressmanager/adapter.purpose: Persistence layer for the progressmanager domain: a Redis-backed implementation of the progressmanager.Adapter interface that stores per-operation progress counters as JSON under namespaced keys with TTL expiration. Also ships noop and testify-mock variants.

patterns.name: Config-validated constructor
patterns.description: New(Config) validates via config.Validate() (Expiration>0, Redis!=nil, Logger!=nil) before constructing the unexported *adapter; never construct the struct directly.
patterns.example: func New(config Config) (progressmanager.Adapter, error) { if err := config.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err } ... }

patterns.name: Interface compile-time assertion
patterns.description: Every concrete type asserts the parent interface: `var _ progressmanager.Adapter = (*adapter)(nil)` and `(*adapterNoop)(nil)`. Add the same line for any new variant.

patterns.name: Validate input before I/O
patterns.description: Each adapter method calls input.Validate() first and wraps the error (fmt.Errorf("validate get progress input: %w", err)) before touching Redis.
patterns.example: if err := input.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, fmt.Errorf("validate get progress input: %w", err) }

patterns.name: redis.Nil -> domain not-found
patterns.description: Reads translate redis.Nil into models.NewGenericNotFoundError; other Redis errors are wrapped verbatim. Never leak redis.Nil upward.
patterns.example: if cmd.Err() == redis.Nil { return nil, models.NewGenericNotFoundError(fmt.Errorf("progress not found for id: %s", input.ProgressID.ID)) }

patterns.name: Namespaced JSON value storage
patterns.description: UpsertProgress json.Marshal's the entity.Progress and SETs it with a.expiration TTL; GetProgress json.Unmarshal's it back. Keys built only via a.getKey(ProgressID).

key_file_guides.role: Config struct + Validate, New() Redis adapter constructor, NewNoop() constructor, struct definitions and interface assertions.
key_file_guides.watch_for: KeyPrefix is optional (empty allowed); Expiration/Redis/Logger are required. Don't add slog.Default() fallbacks — Logger is injected.

key_file_guides.role: Redis Get/Upsert implementations plus getKey() key builder.
key_file_guides.watch_for: getKey concatenates staticKeyPrefix (already ends in ':') with another ':' producing 'progress::ns:id' / '<prefix>:progress::ns:id'. Both branches must stay in sync if the key scheme changes.

key_file_guides.role: adapterNoop methods: GetProgress always returns NotFound, UpsertProgress is a silent success.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Imports entity via the bare 'entity' alias here while progress.go uses the same path — keep noop's GetProgress returning NotFound so disabled progress tracking degrades gracefully.

key_file_guides.role: MockProgressManager (testify mock) implementing progressmanager.Service for service-layer tests.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Mocks the SERVICE interface (not Adapter) and declares DeleteProgressByRuntimeID, which is NOT on the current progressmanager.Service interface — drift; verify against service.go before relying on it.

anti_patterns: Returning redis.Nil to callers instead of models.NewGenericNotFoundError.
Building Redis keys inline instead of through getKey().
Falling back to slog.Default() instead of requiring Config.Logger.

decisions.rationale: Progress is ephemeral, high-churn operation telemetry; TTL expiration auto-evicts stale runs without migrations.

decisions.decision: Noop and mock variants live alongside the real adapter.
decisions.rationale: Lets DI wire a no-op when progress tracking is disabled and lets service tests use testify without Redis.

openmeter/progressmanager/entity.purpose: Domain types and validation rules for the progressmanager package: ProgressID, Progress, and the GetProgressInput/UpsertProgressInput method-input wrappers. Pure data + Validate(), no I/O.

patterns.name: errors.Join validation accumulation
patterns.description: Every Validate() collects into `var errs []error` and returns errors.Join(errs...) — it never returns on the first failure. Nested validators are wrapped with field context.
patterns.example: if err := a.NamespacedModel.Validate(); err != nil { errs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf("namespaced model: %w", err)) }

patterns.name: Embedded ID + NamespacedModel
patterns.description: ProgressID embeds models.NamespacedModel (json inline) plus an ID string; Progress embeds ProgressID; inputs embed the domain type they carry. Reuse embedding rather than duplicating fields.
patterns.example: type Progress struct { ProgressID `json:"id"`; Success uint64 ... }

patterns.name: Counter invariants in Validate
patterns.description: Progress.Validate enforces Success+Failed<=Total, zero counters when Total==0, and non-zero UpdatedAt. New counter fields must extend these checks.
patterns.example: if a.Success+a.Failed > a.Total { errs = append(errs, errors.New("success and failed must be less than or equal to total")) }

key_file_guides.role: Defines ProgressID, Progress, GetProgressInput, UpsertProgressInput and their Validate() methods.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Receivers are named `a` despite the types not being adapters; UpdatedAt is required (IsZero rejected). Input wrappers exist purely to keep method signatures stable as fields grow.

anti_patterns: Returning on the first validation error instead of accumulating with errors.Join.
Adding counter fields without updating the Success+Failed<=Total / Total==0 invariants.
Importing anything beyond pkg/models here — this folder must stay I/O-free.

decisions.decision: Method inputs are dedicated structs (GetProgressInput/UpsertProgressInput) wrapping the domain type.
decisions.rationale: Keeps adapter/service signatures stable and gives each call its own Validate() seam.

code_examples.scenario: Build and validate an upsert input

openmeter/progressmanager/httpdriver.purpose: HTTP transport layer for the progressmanager domain: wraps progressmanager.Service in httptransport handlers, decoding the namespace from context and mapping entity.Progress to api.Progress. Read-only (GetProgress) endpoint surface.

patterns.name: Nested Handler interface + handler struct
patterns.description: Handler embeds ProgressHandler which exposes GetProgress() GetProgressHandler; the unexported handler{service, namespaceDecoder, options} implements it, asserted via `var _ Handler = (*handler)(nil)`.

patterns.name: httptransport.NewHandlerWithArgs three-func shape
patterns.description: Each endpoint = (decode req from *http.Request + path arg) + (logic calling h.service) + JSONResponseEncoderWithStatus, with WithOperationName appended via httptransport.AppendOptions(h.options, ...).

patterns.name: Namespace from decoder, not request body
patterns.description: resolveNamespace pulls the namespace via namespaceDecoder.GetNamespace(ctx); a missing namespace is a 500 commonhttp.NewHTTPError, never trusted from the client.
patterns.example: ns, ok := h.namespaceDecoder.GetNamespace(ctx); if !ok { return "", commonhttp.NewHTTPError(http.StatusInternalServerError, errors.New("internal server error")) }

patterns.name: Type-alias request/response/handler triplet
patterns.description: Requests reuse entity types (GetProgressRequest = entity.GetProgressInput), responses reuse api types (GetProgressResponse = api.Progress), handler aliases httptransport.HandlerWithArgs[...]. Domain->API mapping via local progressToAPI.

key_file_guides.role: Handler/ProgressHandler interfaces, handler struct, New(namespaceDecoder, service, options...), resolveNamespace helper.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Missing namespace returns 500 (internal), not 400 — it's expected to be set by upstream middleware.

key_file_guides.role: GetProgress() handler implementation and progressToAPI mapper.
key_file_guides.watch_for: service.GetProgress returning a nil *Progress with no error is treated as an internal error (fmt.Errorf), not 404 — 404 must come from the adapter's NotFound. progressToAPI drops namespace/ID (api.Progress only carries counters + UpdatedAt).

anti_patterns: Reading the namespace from the request body/query instead of namespaceDecoder.
Hand-rolling http.ResponseWriter encoding instead of commonhttp JSON encoders.
Omitting WithOperationName, which OpenAPI/operation routing relies on.
Mapping entity->API outside a dedicated *ToAPI function.

decisions.decision: Only GetProgress is exposed over HTTP; UpsertProgress is internal.
decisions.rationale: Progress is written by background streaming/clickhouse jobs, not clients; the API is read-only polling of operation status.

code_examples.scenario: Map a domain Progress to the API type
